description: Having a sharp, pungent taste that is not sweet; often unpleasant but sometimes valued. 'Puko' names the quality of bitterness—the taste that warns, that wakes, that medicines often carry.
sound_symbolism: The word puckers. 'Pu' begins with slight rejection, taste encountered. Then 'ko' catches sharply, bitterness confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'puko nepha' (bitter medicine). Opposite of 'kaelo' (sweet); neighbor of 'tuko' (sour). Can describe taste or metaphorical bitterness of experience.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Bitter experiences can teach—difficulty sometimes offers lessons sweetness cannot.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Bitter tastes often signaled medicine—healing herbs frequently carried sharp, unpleasant flavors.
